results.possibleIdentity: Anthony E. Troianello was an electrical engineer active from at least the late 1960s through the early 2000s. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he was employed by Mepco Inc. (Manufacturer of Electronic Products Corporation) in Florham Park, New Jersey, where he specialized in electrical engineering and electronic components. He was a registered participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaboration between the technical and artistic communities. Troianello's technical expertise later transitioned to Vishay Intertechnology, Inc. (following Vishay's acquisition of Mepco/Centralab), where he became a prominent inventor. He holds several patents, notably for the development of electro-pyrotechnic initiators used in automotive 'smart' airbag systems and other specialized electronic components. Personal records from the 1970s place him in Madison, New Jersey, a community adjacent to Florham Park.
